rationale: Web exposes paymentMethodOrder as integrator-configurable prop. Mobile only has hardcoded priorityArr sort, no merchant-configurable ordering option.

evidence: [{"name":"terms","file":"src/Types/PaymentType.res","snippet":"options.terms: {card, auBecsDebit, bancontact, ideal, sepaDebit, sofort, usBankAccount}: Auto|Always|Never"}]
rationale: Web SDK exposes per-payment-method terms visibility config (auto/always/never). Mobile SDK has hardcoded terms text but no integrator config to control display. Minor integrator customization gap.

wallets_ui_config is a web-specific UI customization for wallet button styling. Mobile SDK renders wallet buttons via native platform APIs (Apple Pay/Google Pay), not configurable web-style UI config.

customMethodNames is a web SDK config prop allowing integrators to rename payment methods in the UI. Mobile SDK has no equivalent — it renders methods dynamically from backend with no client-side aliasing.
Config flag sdk_handle_confirm_payment not found in mobile SDK. Mobile has confirmPayment flow but lacks this integrator config toggle present in web's PaymentType.res.
No hide_expired_payment_methods config or alias found. Mobile stores expiry data but lacks a config toggle to filter expired saved cards from display.
Mobile has NickNameElement but no hide_card_nickname_field config prop. Visibility is controlled by displaySavedPaymentMethodsCheckbox instead of a dedicated toggle.
Mobile shows card terms via hardcoded locale strings (cardTermsPart1/2) but lacks a config prop for integrators to customize the message, unlike web's custom_message_for_card_terms.
Mobile has surcharge support (show_surcharge_breakup_screen, surcharge_details) but no show_short_surcharge_message config toggle. Minor UI display option absent from mobile.
read_only config prop not found in any ReScript source. It's a minor integrator config for disabling form inputs, not critical to payment flow.

evidence: [{"name":"element_style_variables","file":"src/Types/ElementType.res","snippet":"styleClass: {backgroundColor, color, fontFamily, fontSize, iconColor, padding, hover?, focus?, ...}"}]
rationale: element_style_variables is a web CSS custom properties concept (ElementType.res). Mobile uses native React Native styling via ThemeContext/ThemebasedStyle — CSS variables don't apply to native platforms.
No allows_delayed_payment_methods config prop or alias found in web SDK. Mobile-specific config for controlling delayed payment method behavior.
Mobile SDK config prop to globally filter payment methods needing shipping. Web handles shipping per-method (GPay/ApplePay configs). Not applicable as a global toggle in web.
enablePartialLoading is a mobile-specific config for incremental UI rendering. Web SDK uses static bundled components, no equivalent exists or is needed.
Mobile SDK config letting host app hide built-in confirm button. Web SDK manages its own pay button internally; no equivalent prop exists or is needed.
netcetera_sdk_api_key is a native 3DS SDK config. Web handles 3DS via browser redirects, not native SDK init — no need for this key.
Web SDK uses clientSecret for auth; ephemeral key is a mobile-specific pattern for customer session auth in native SDKs, not applicable to web.
No CustomWidget component or widgets directory exists in web SDK. Feature appears to be a mobile-specific custom rendering widget with no web equivalent.
FormViewJourney is a web-only payout collect widget UI component managing multi-step form navigation. Mobile uses React Native navigation and backend-driven dynamic forms instead.

evidence: [{"name":"click_to_pay","file":"src/Components/ClickToPayUiComponents.res","snippet":"Click to Pay widget (auth, details, learn-more, not-you screens merged)"}]
rationale: Click to Pay is a web-only Mastercard/Visa JS SDK widget (iframe-based). Native mobile SDKs use platform-specific wallets (Apple Pay, Google Pay) instead.
InfoElement is a web-specific UI helper for displaying info text near form fields. Mobile uses native RN primitives; no equivalent wrapper needed.

evidence: [{"name":"installment_options","file":"src/Components/InstallmentOptions.res","snippet":"Installment plan selector widget (options, item, plan details merged)"}]
rationale: Mobile has installment_payment_enabled flag in API types but no InstallmentOptions UI component. Web has dedicated component for selecting installment plans; mobile lacks equivalent UI.
ScanCardButton uses device camera/OCR to scan physical cards — a native mobile capability. Web browsers lack this API; web SDK has no equivalent and shouldn't.
